titleStmt.title: SII 28.316: original edition by J. Sundaram
No. 316. (A.R. No. 470 of 1913.)

summary.p: The begining and end of the lines as also the continuation of the inscription have been lost. Even in the available portion, the stone has flaked off resulting in the loss of a few lines. However, the records is interesting in so far as it refers to aṟudi-kkarai (i.e.) the final settlement in the process of rotation of blocs of land among the holders of shares in the village.

The record says that Naṭṭūrāṇḍāṉ, a nāṭṭu-kkāmiṇḍar, who probably belonged to Vaḍakarai-nāḍu endowed a lamp and deposited 10 paṇam with the ūr and ūrāḷigaḷ of Kūvalūr in Kāñchikkūval-nāḍu. The latter, on accepting the endowed amount, allotted against it some land (taṟai) as aṟudi-kkaṟai (final allotment without the liablility to be changed at the next reallocation-final karai-of the blocs of land in the village). The said land was given to the charge of a śivabrāhmaṇa of Bhāradvāja-gōtram named Āvuḍaiyār Nāyaka-nambiyār alias Periyanāṭṭu-bhaṭṭar.

There are nine other fragments of at least three other inscriptions. One of them quotes the year 10 of a king (name lost), another a gift for a twilight lamp (sandhyā-dīpam) and another a gift of 10 paṇam for a lamp.

revisionDesc.change: Conversion of digital encoding to DHARMA encoding scheme according to EGD (Encoding Guide for Diplomatic Editions)

div.p:
śrīnanmaṅkilañciṟṟakaApparently this is a corruption of ciṟakka . śrīcuntirapāṇṭiya tevaṟku yāṇṭu yiraṇṭāvatu kāñcikkūval nāṭṭuk kūvalūrurumurāḷikaḷom vaṭakarai nāṭṭuk karuvaḷi This portion of the inscription is erased.ṭaikaḷil nāṭṭukkāmiṇṭar naṭṭurāṇṭāṉeṉ kūSome portion of the inscription in continuation of lines 1-3 and a few letters at the beginning of the lines have been lost.
māka Oru viḷakku vaikkaiyil yivviḷakku yivar kaiyyāṟ paṇam 10�kaikkoṇṭu yippaṇam pattukkum cemmaṇ kuḻiyil Ūṟpaṭṭi Oreṟa ^2^ kayum Aṟutikkaṟaik kellaiyāvatu kiḻakku Ūṟpaṭṭikku meṟkum teṟku va
kappaṭṭikku vaṭakkum meṟku Attāṇi vaḻiyiṭṭalukkuk kiḻakkum vaṭakkuk kaṇiya māṭiyiṭṭalukkut teṟkum Innānkellaikkuṭpaṭṭa taṟai ponnuṟak koṇṭu yinnilamaṟuti kuṭuttom kūvalūrom ka �ṇṭāṉukku kāñcik kūval nāṭṭuk kūvaContinuation lost. There are nine fragments of inscriptions in the same place which read as follows (see next page).
kāṇiyuṭaiya śivabrāhmaṇan bhāradvājagotrattu Āvuṭaiyār nāyaka nampiyārāna periyanāṭṭup paṭṭaṟku nāṭṭuk kāmiṇṭa naṭṭurāṇṭānen cemmaṇkuḻiyiṟ paṭṭi Aṟutikkāṇṭu kuṭukkaiyil In nilaṅ kaikkoṇṭu Uṭaiyār naṭṭu rāṇṭāContinuation lost. There are nine fragments of inscriptions in the same place which read as follows (see next page).

tevaṟku yāṇṭu pattāvatu
lil veḷḷāḻan cempa

māci mātam mutal kāñcikkūval nāṭṭik
kaḷil Āṇṭān maṉaikkiḻatti kovā

caṅkoṇṭu kūval nā
Ittaṟmam ṟuvān vaḻiyeḻaccamaṟuva

ṉa nāyinār �ṇṭāṟ kucca yāti
ten Ikkā ṇan
ḷa nāyakaren cantiyātipa viḷakko
k koṉṟuṅ kuṭaṅ koṇṭu koyil pukuvān

ṇṭiye
ṅkuṭut
ṭaiya piḷḷai
ṇṭen yivi

ḷakkoṉṟup paṇam pattu
�śa kottirattil va
ṉṟukkum paṇam pattuṅ ke
ntirātitta varai celuttu
Digital edition of SII 28.316 by automatically converted to DHARMA conventions.
